Motion To Seal Two Confidential Documents Filed By Plaintiff In Connection With Plaintiffs Opposition To Defendants Motion To Disqualify
Off Calendar May-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS OURARING INC. AND OURA HEALTH LTD.'S MOTION TO SEAL TWO CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS FILED BY PLAINTIFF IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISQUALIFY

Appearances:
Diane Cafferata and Alex Choi of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, LLP (213.443.3000, dianecafferata@quinnemanuel.com, alexchoi@quinnemanuel.com) for defendants and moving parties;
Jenna Farleigh and Julian Schneider of Susman Godfrey LLP (206.516.3823, jfarleigh@susmangodfrey.com, jschneider@susmangodfrey.com) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Discussed. Plaintiffs raise no objection to granting the motion. The court rules as follows:
Defendants Ouraring Inc. and Oura Health Ltd.'s (collectively Oura) motion to seal Exhibits 2 and 4 to Plaintiffs' Unopposed Notice Of Lodging Documents Conditionally Under Seal In Support Of Its Opposition To Defendants' Motion To Disqualify filed on April 28, 2026 is granted. Plaintiffs waived statutory notice of this motion at the noticed hearing date. Oura's factual showing establishes that these exhibits contain confidential business information whose exposure would give rise to competitive harm. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR 10619 (925.384.0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com)
